title: Operator confirmation of machine control scheme
abstract: A customizable control scheme determines operations of a machine or attached implements that result from usage of machine controls by an operator. A display of the machine presents a control scheme confirmation screen in response to the machine turning on, a new implement being attached to the machine, or another trigger condition. An operator can learn the control scheme that the machine is currently configured to use via the control scheme confirmation screen, and provide user input confirming that the operator is aware of the control scheme that the machine is currently configured to use prior to beginning active operations of the machine following the trigger condition.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A computer-implemented method, comprising: causing (404), by a processor (502) of an on-board computing system (108) of a machine (100), a display (112) of the machine (100) to present a control scheme confirmation screen (116), wherein: the control scheme confirmation screen (116) visually depicts a control scheme that, based on control scheme data (110), is currently configured to be used to translate input data from machine controls (106) of the machine (100) into corresponding operations of the machine (100); receiving (412), by the processor (502), and via the control scheme confirmation screen (116), user input comprising a confirmation of the control scheme; and enabling (414), by the processor (502), and based on the user input, active machine operations of the machine (100) that are controlled via the machine controls (106) based on the control scheme data (110). 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein the active machine operations of the machine (100), controlled via the machine controls (106) based on the control scheme data (110), comprise at least one of: driving operations of the machine (100), or implement operations of an implement (102) attached to the machine (100). 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: detecting, by the processor (502), an occurrence of a trigger condition associated with the control scheme confirmation screen (116); preventing, by the processor (502), the active machine operations based on the occurrence of the trigger condition until the user input comprising the confirmation of the control scheme is received; and 

 causing, by the processor, the control scheme confirmation screen (116) to be presented based on the occurrence of the trigger condition. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 3, wherein the trigger condition comprises at least one of: a machine-on event (402) associated with the machine (100) being turned on, or an implement change event (416) associated with a new implement (102) being attached to the machine (100). 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 4, further comprising: determining, by the processor (502), an identifier of the new implement (102); and determining, by the processor (502), the control scheme associated with the new implement (102) based on the identifier of the new implement (102), wherein the control scheme confirmation screen (116) visually depicts the control scheme associated with the new implement (102). 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein: the user input comprises a first user input, the method further comprises: receiving (408), by the processor (502), and via the control scheme confirmation screen, (116) a second user input indicating a requested change to the control scheme; and changing (410), by the processor (502), the control scheme data (110) based on the second user input, and the first user input confirms the control scheme based at least in part on the changing of the control scheme data (110) based on the second user input. 

 7. A machine (100) comprising: a transmission (104); an implement (102) configured to perform at least one work task; machine controls (106) configured to, based on a control scheme defined by control scheme data (110), control operations of at least one of the transmission (104) or the implement (102); a display (112); and an on-board computing system (108) configured to: cause (404) the display (112) to present a control scheme confirmation screen (116) that visually depicts the control scheme; receive (412), via the control scheme confirmation screen (116), user input comprising a confirmation of the control scheme; and enable (414), based on the user input, active machine operations of the at least one of the transmission (104) or the implement (102) that are controlled via the machine controls (106) based on the control scheme data (106). 8. The machine (100) of claim 7, wherein the on-board computing system (108) is further configured to: detect an occurrence of a trigger condition associated with the control scheme confirmation screen (116), prevent the active machine operations based on the occurrence of the trigger condition until the user input comprising the confirmation of the control scheme is received, and cause the control scheme confirmation screen (116) to be presented based on the occurrence of the trigger condition. 9. The machine (100) of claim 7, wherein the trigger condition comprises at least one of: a machine-on event (402) associated with the machine (100) being turned on, or 

 an implement change event (416) associated with the implement (102) being attached to the machine (100). 10. The machine (100) of claim 7, wherein: the user input comprises a first user input, the on-board computing system (108) is further configured to: receive, via the control scheme confirmation screen (116), a second user input indicating a requested change to the control scheme; and change the control scheme data (110) based on the second user input, and the first user input confirms the control scheme based at least in part on changing of the control scheme based on the second user input.
